title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2		Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Terminology
AFT
Automated Funds Transfer.
Biller Identification Number (BID)
The Biller Identification Number (BID) is an identification number used to access Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payments System. In Ontario, the BID is composed of a sequence of nine numerical characters. In BC, the BID is a number that ranges from one to five digits.
Biller
A biller is an entity set up on Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payments System to receive customer-initiated bill payments.
CCIN
Corporate Creditor Identification Number.
CGI
Consulting Group International (CGI) owns and operates the RFS/NS banking system.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications.
Electronic Bill Payments System
Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payments System allows participating financial institutions to submit customer-initiated bill payments to Central 1 for further distribution to participating billers.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
IVR
Interactive Voice Response (IVR) is a method of making electronic bill payments via telephone.
OLT
The Online Tracing System (OLT) is an application that allows authorized users to create bill payment trace requests and view biller-initiated trace requests.
Restricted Entity
A restricted entity is an entity involved in one or more of the following:
Money services businesses, including currency exchanges
Money transmitters and casas de cambios, including payment services providers
Casinos and gambling establishments
Internet gambling/gaming associated businesses and websites
Marijuana providers and/or distributors
Virtual currency exchangers and administrators
RFS/NT
Retail Financial System New Technology (RFS/NT) is a banking system owned and operated by CGI.
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
SRD
The Secure Report Distribution (SRD) system allows authorized users to access pre-defined reports via the Internet. Billers use SRD to access their electronic bill payment reports.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users.
User Management Security Officer
User Management Security Officers use the User Management to add, modify, and delete Client Centre Users and to modify and delete other User Management Security Officers. User Management Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications.
Vendor
See Biller.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
Electronic Bill Payments System Enrollment
Question 1
Is there a fee for adding a biller to Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payments System?
Answer
No. Central 1 doesnât charge a fee for adding a biller to the EBP system.
Question 2
Who can participate in Central 1âs Electronic Bill Payments System?
Answer
Central 1âs policy requires that a biller must be registered with a lead bank. This ensures due diligence and increases Central 1âs level of security. If your financial institution is the lead bank for the biller, Central 1 can set up the biller on the system with minimal effort. If a biller is registered with a different lead bank (e.g., Royal Bank), the biller should initiate enrollment through that financial institution. The billerâs lead bank can send the enrollment information to Central 1 to facilitate the billerâs set-up with Central 1 and other financial institutions.
Question 3
What is an account validation rule?
Answer
This is the account number format that a biller uses to identify its customer.
Question 4
Why does a biller need to provide an account validation rule?
Answer
An account validation rule ensures that the biller only receives payments from valid customer accounts and assists in applying the payments.
Question 5
What options does a biller have for selecting account validation rules?
Answer
An account validation rule is selected as follows:
All alpha characters, or
All numeric digits, or
Combination of alpha and numeric
Minimum length
Maximum length
Fixed length
The length of the account number canât exceed 20 characters.
A biller also has the option of including a special check digit formula (e.g., Luhnâs Mod 10). With this option, the biller must provide a detailed description of how the check-digit is calculated and six valid sample account numbers.
Question 6
How does a biller identify its account validation rules?
Answer
The biller must complete âAccount Validation Rulesâ on the Biller Enrollment Request, Form 1320.
END
